title: Pipe repair system
abstract: The present invention provides an adjustable clamp apparatus having curved arms which pivot/articulate relative to each other. The distance between pivot points can be adjusted via specially configured couplers having nuts and threaded rods. The curved arms can be locked together with a rod placed in a slot, and then squeezed by rotation of threaded rod. In the interior of the curved arms there can be a plurality of projections or contact points. A strap can be connected to a selected adjustable clamp (e.g., by a pair of padeyes) and used to raise and lower the clamp. When being lowered onto a pipe to be repaired, the curved arms can be open wide and when their upper portions make contact with the pipe to be repaired, this contact will tend to cause arms to close around the pipe relative to each other. Once clamped against the pipe with the clamps, the repair members can be welded together.
claims: Claims 1. A method of repairing a pipe section that is damaged comprising the steps of: a) providing one or more repair members, each having an inner curved surface that conforms generally to at least part of the outer surface of the pipe section; b) providing one or more clamps, each including first and second pairs of curved members and two couplers that each connect to both pairs of curved sections, said couplers being positioned at spaced apart positions; c) wherein each coupler includes an externally threaded member and first and second internally threaded nuts, at least one said nut forming a threaded connection with the externally threaded member; d) tightening the clamp or clamps to compress the repair member against the pipe section; and e) wherein in step "d" one or both of the externally threaded members is rotated to change the distance between the nuts. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising repeating steps "b" through "e" at a second position that is spaced from said first position. 3. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 1, wherein the curved members of each pair are spaced apart. 4. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 3, wherein the curved members of each pair are generally parallel. 5. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 1, wherein the curved members of each pair have spaced part ends, at least one said end having a slot and step "b" includes attaching a said nut to two of said curved members at said slots. 6. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 3, further comprising the step of spacing the curved members apart with one or more transverse plates. 7. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 1, wherein one or more of the nuts are pivotally attached to the curved members. 8. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 5, wherein one or more of the nuts are pivotally attached to the curved members. 9. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 5, wherein one or more of the nuts are both pivotally attached to the curved members and removably attachable to the curved members at a said slot. 10. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 1, wherein in step "b" wherein the clamps have concave surfaces with one or more projections. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein step "d" includes tightening one or more jacks mounted on said clamps. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the jacks are hydraulic jacks. 13. A method of repairing a pipe section that is damaged comprising the steps of: a) providing one or more repair members, each having an inner curved surface that conforms generally to the outer surface of the pipe section; b) providing one or more clamps, each including first and second pairs of arc shaped members and two couplers that each connect to both pairs of arc shaped members, said couplers being positioned at spaced apart positions; c) wherein each coupler includes an externally threaded member and nuts that each form a connection with a said externally threaded member, one said nut forming a threaded connection a said externally threaded member; d) tightening the clamp or clamps to compress the repair member against the pipe section; e) wherein in step "d" one or both of the externally threaded members is rotated, which changes the distance between two of the said nuts; f) repeating step "d" at a second position that is spaced from said first position. 14. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 13, wherein the curved members of each pair are spaced apart and generally parallel. 15. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 14, wherein the curved members of each pair are in generally parallel planes. 16. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 13, wherein the curved members of each pair have spaced part ends, at least one said end having a slot and step "b" includes attaching a said nut to a said curved member at a said slot. 17. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 14, further comprising the step of spacing the curved members apart with one or more transverse plates. 18. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 13, wherein one or more of the nuts are pivotally attached to the curved members. 19. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 16, wherein one or more of the nuts are pivotally attached to the curved members. 20. The method of repairing a pipe section of claim 16, wherein each of the couplers is pivotally attached to a pair of the curved members and removably attachable to a second pair of the curved members at said slots. 21. A pipe repair apparatus for covering a damaged section of pipe, comprising: a) first and second curved repair sections, each having opposed curved edges and opposed longitudinally extending edges; b) one or more clamps, each having pivotally connected clamps sections; c) a pair of couplers, each forming a pivotal connection with each clamp section; and d) each coupler having first and second nuts that define a point of said pivotal connection and a threaded shaft that engages an internally threaded portion of one said nut. 22. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21, wherein the shaft has a head. 23. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 22, wherein the shaft has an unthreaded portion next to the head. 24. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21 , wherein the shaft forms a threaded connection with one said nut and an unthreaded engagement with another said nut. 25. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21, wherein each clamp section is comprised of a pair of curved plates connected with a plurality of transverse plates. 26. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21 , wherein one of said curved plates has a slot that connects with a said nut to form a pivotal connection. 27. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21 , wherein each clamp section has a concavely shaped inner surface having one or more convex projections. 28. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21 , further comprising weldments that secure one repair member to the other repair member. 29. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 21, further comprising one or more jacks mounted on at least one clamp and positioned to direct pressure against one or both of the repair sections. 30. The pipe repair apparatus of claim 29, wherein the jacks are hydraulic jacks. 31. The method and apparatus as substantially disclosed and/or described.
